source.title: Understanding and sharing intentions: the origins of cultural cognition

confidence_rationale: Landmark BBS target article establishing shared intentionality as the foundation of human cultural cognition, including games and social institutions. High source quality. Mapping fidelity is high for ARC-049 and MECH-197's claim that play type progression tracks shared intentionality development. Transfer risk is low. Slightly reduced by the generality of the claim -- shared intentionality underlies all human collaborative practice, making specific predictions for games-with-rules architecture require extrapolation.
failure_signatures: Theoretical review -- not direct empirical test of games-with-rules specifically.
Developmental timeline (14 months for first shared intentionality) does not directly specify the games-with-rules emergence timeline.
Cannot speak to Q-035 (continuous vs. discrete monitoring) from this level of theoretical analysis.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Shared intentionality -- joint goals and attention with mutual understanding of individual roles and perspectives -- is the species-unique cognitive infrastructure enabling human cultural cognition including games, social norms, and institutions. It develops in two intertwined pathways beginning in the first 14 months.
mapping.ree_translation: Shared intentionality is the cognitive substrate for ARC-049's bilateral frame. The progression from joint attention (12 months) to joint goals/roles in collaborative activities (24 months) to normative rule games (36-60 months) matches MECH-197's play type progression. Each play type requires and recruits richer shared intentionality: sensorimotor play needs joint attention only, pretend play needs shared counterfactual premises, games-with-rules need agent-neutral normative shared goals. DEV-NEED-014 (cooperative play as a training objective) maps to the multi-agent full bilateral ARC-049 that shared intentionality theory predicts as the developmental endpoint.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper establishes shared intentionality as a general cognitive capacity, not a specific architecture for play frame maintenance. REE's mapping from shared intentionality theory to ARC-049's specific bilateral frame mechanism requires an architectural translation that the paper does not perform.
mapping.source_context: BBS target article with extensive commentary. Review of comparative (great apes vs. humans) and developmental psychology evidence.

confidence_components.notes: Landmark paper with very high citation count. Mapping fidelity reduced by generality -- the paper grounds the whole domain of shared intentionality, not games-with-rules specifically.
